rulings.Case Title: ALYSSA MUELLER VS. ELITE INVESTMENT & CAPITAL GROUP INC. A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: [Corrected] Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 23, 2022, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Relief from Waiver of Objections is OFF CALENDAR for department 501; to be heard in department 301 at 9:00 a.m. on December 23, 2022. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ARMANINO FAMILY LLC, VS. PAUL ANTHONY BALLESTRASSE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended And Supplemental Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 23, 2022, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File a Second Amended and Supplemental Complaint is continued to January 10, 2022. No later than December 29, 2022 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 1 page) clarifying whether Paul Ballesrasse, individually, and Joanne Karetzky are named as defendants in the first cause of action for contribution. If they are, is Plaintiff seeking contribution jointly and severally from all three defendants, from Paul and Joanne in equal shares, etc. Plaintiff shall further address in the brief citing case authority (not to exceed 2 pages) whether this Court may determine the interests in the property for beneficiaries of an undistributed Trust and if such determination is in the purview of the Probate Court re: timely distribution of trust assets, etc. Defendant may file a response to the supplemental brief no later than January 5, 2023. The brief shall not exceed the page limits listed above. Plaintiff shall serve a copy of the motion, opposition, reply and supplemental brief of Ms. Karetzky and her counsel. Proof of service to be filed no later than December 29, 2022. Defendant shall serve a copy of their supplemental response on Ms. Karetzky at the time of the filing. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RPSRRS, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILTIY COMPANY VS. DANIEL PADILLA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, December 23, 2022, line 4. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. The Court will not consider a declaration on the demurrer. Defendant failed to cite any authority for the proposition that "he landlord must bear the burden to determine whether nonpayment resulted from a COVID-19 related income loss" to state a claim for unlawful detainer. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)
